articles.title: Build Better AI Agents: 5 Developer Tips from the Agent Bake-Off
articles.snippet: The Google Cloud AI Agent Bake-Off highlights a shift from simple prompt engineering to rigorous agentic engineering, emphasizing that production-ready AI requires a modular, multi-agent architecture. The post outlines five key developer tips, including decomposing complex tasks into specialized sub-agents and using deterministic code for execution to prevent probabilistic errors. Furthermore, it advises developers to prioritize multimodality and open-source protocols like MCP to ensure agents a
articles.ai_summary: The Google Cloud AI Agent Bake-Off emphasizes the need for a modular, multi-agent architecture in AI development. It provides five tips for developers to enhance AI agents, focusing on specialized sub-agents and deterministic code to improve reliability.
articles.why_it_matters: Enhances AI development practices for more robust applications.

articles.title: Get ready for Google I/O: Livestream schedule revealed
articles.snippet: Google I/O returns May 19–20 to showcase major updates in AI, Android, Chrome, and Cloud, beginning with a keynote on the "agentic era" of development. The event will focus on new tools designed to automate complex workflows and simplify the creation of high-quality, AI-ready applications. Attendees can register to access live sessions, technical demos, and professional development resources both live and on-demand.
articles.ai_summary: Google I/O is set for May 19-20, featuring major updates in AI and other technologies. The event will include a keynote on the 'agentic era' and showcase new tools for automating workflows.
